title: helianthemum canum subsp. balcanicum
intro: Helianthemum canum subsp. balcanicum, also known as the rockrose or Balkan rockrose, is a low-growing, shrubby plant that belongs to the family Cistaceae and is native to Southeastern Europe. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Helianthemum canum subsp. balcanicum include its potential to treat various conditions such as wounds, ulcers, and skin irritations, as well as its use in traditional medicine to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the plant are typically used to make infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
